title: Asking BU students which major is the biggest waste of money 🗑️#students #bostonuniversity
body:
I got a bachelors in criminal justice and ended up not using it. Left the police academy cause it ended up not being my thing, plus it was 72k a year (not bad)
Left for this trucking company. In 2-3 months I got my CDL at a local trucking school, ended up with a six figure trucking job. Sometimes I feel like I wasted those 4 years going to college but it is what it is
author: @Chris_Trucker

title: Asking BU students which major is the biggest waste of money 🗑️#students #bostonuniversity
body: I'm a journalism major with a minor in communications. I was already good at writing but my courses prep me more for the social aspect of journalism. Like interviews, getting good sources, fact checking, how to think and write critically, and how to talk to people professionally. That stuff is gonna take me farther than just my writing will, and it would've taken me alot of trial and error if I don't learn it in college.
author: @OHGEEBEE

title: Asking BU students which major is the biggest waste of money 🗑️#students #bostonuniversity
body:
BFA in fine arts is actually really useful if you use it. Art school gives you tons of connections and access to contemporaries (profs and peers) as well as access to different media like printing labs, photography studios, and 3D shops. Yeah, you can be successful in the arts independently, but schooling really does help with connections and development of truly good art, both conceptually and technically, that is worthy of galleries and exhibitions. Doing it on your own takes a lot of active seeking out of peers and tools for learning, development, and inspiration.

I’m honestly so sick of people saying art degrees are worthless or that the arts are worthless because they think you can’t make money, which is false … plus, culture and social progression is literally driven by the arts soooo …
author: @Skallanni

title: Asking BU students which major is the biggest waste of money 🗑️#students #bostonuniversity
body: No major is useless. You have to make connections and network BEFORE you graduate to immediately start succeeding in your chosen field. If you only get the degree but have no network, you're going to take way longer to climb the career ladder. Do internships!

No regrets.

body: Some majors provide a more direct line to jobs (engineering, education/teaching, computer science, etc.) but any major can get you a job - the difference is often how hard you have to work to make yourself a more competitive candidate in the field. title: if MIT were honest  #americancollege #mit #college #collegeadmissions #ivyleague
body:
“weird and quirky, lol, random xD”
actual things at mit:
- clothing optional dorm floors with “exercise poles” installed
- explosives in the dorm courtyard
- annual “virgin sacrifice to the dark lord krotus” themed party
- wrestling in a pit of glitter lube or fake blood with a tesla coil blasting music
- annual piano drop, where a piano is dropped off of the roof
- real clubs include mixology, chocolate science, spinning balls of fire around your body as a performance art, and something called “assassins’ guild”
- decades old milk (the dorm pet)
- multiple dorms where students can paint literally whatever they want — from renditions of renoir to explicit materials to a fully black hallway with red lights
- before my time but a dorm floor once got in trouble for making a giant concrete replica of a bomb to promote a party called “drink ‘til you drop”
- also before my time, over half the lsd on the east coast was once made in an mit dorm
- also also before my time but us copyright law had to be rewritten because of an undergrad’s pet project pirating a ton of books and stuff

measuring bridges in smoots and putting police cars on the roof is honestly kinda tame for us tbh
author: @kimcoleeppling8177

title: Taking Notes on the 2018 iPad Pro! (Boston College)
body:
Notability feels like a better writing experience when I use the Apple Pencil in comparison with GoodNotes. But Good Notes is organized in a way I like better. Good Notes also works better when it comes to telling where pages end in case you want to send people your notes. I tend to use Good Notes for actual Note taking and Notability for homework assignments.

I’ve been unhappy with nearly all other iPad Pro reviews I’ve been seeing and so I almost skipped this video. I’m glad I did click it though because this video was really good. Plus Harris is attractive so it’s always nice to see him. 😉

title: HARVARD PRE-MED *REALISTIC* DAY-IN-MY-LIFE
body:
As a US med student at a prestigious program, now halfway through my third year, I’ll just share a little unsolicited advice. Make sure to take time for yourself and enjoy your undergraduate experience. Kudos to you for putting in all this work in extracurriculars and resumé building; it’s clear that you’re highly motivated. I will say, though, that a career in medicine is a LONG road and you can only go 100mph for so long before burning out.

The field of medicine is full of neuroticism which applies constant needless pressure to be the best, but I promise you it will be fine and work out well in the end. Take time for yourself: get enough sleep, watch a new series, hang out with friends, visit family. Taking care of yourself in these ways is essential because, otherwise, you may become resentful or regret going into the field.

I also promise that there will be enough time for the essentials (GPA, MCAT prep, research, clinical experience, applications, etc), and you will be more successful in them if you’re healthy in mind and body. Sorry for the rant and I wish the best for you and your future career in our field!
author: @ToastyMuffinz

author: @milo__thefish

title: HARVARD PRE-MED *REALISTIC* DAY-IN-MY-LIFE
body:
It may be easier said than done, but please prioritize your overall health (physical, mental, and emotional). If you are not careful, the load of college and trying to be on top of things chains you. I remember during undergrad and graduate school, I developed gastritis because I was not eating well. I would only get 3 hours of sleep per night. My mental health sank. I had panic attacks to the point I thought I was experiencing a heart attack. I went to the clinic, and they had to send me to the hospital.

Something someone told me, “Do you think your college degree is going to help you when you are a patient in the hospital or six feet under? No. Try creating a balance”.

Please try taking care of yourself now, or else spend the remainder trying to fix it.
author: @donelmalahoo6683

title: HARVARD PRE-MED *REALISTIC* DAY-IN-MY-LIFE
body: This reminds me of my junior/senior years in high school. It was just go go go no breaks gotta keep up with homework, Extracurriculars, work, internship, personal projects, etc. It was absolutely draining. I’m halfway through the first year of college and I no longer stress myself out like that. It’s not worth it. I can still achieve success leading a slower life
author: @audreyguo13

title: HARVARD PRE-MED *REALISTIC* DAY-IN-MY-LIFE
body: I am a final year med student who used to work just like that. The chronic stress gave me a brutal adrenal insufficiency diagnosis. So watch out to your sleep and overall health
author: @SaraKho

title: HARVARD PRE-MED *REALISTIC* DAY-IN-MY-LIFE
body: I‘m a German med studeng and I’m so happy to not have to live like this. I work hard as well, but I keep an eye on sleeping enough and I always keep my sundays study free. Wouldn’t want to risk my health and well being for a prestigious uni😢
author: @elske7736

title: HARVARD PRE-MED *REALISTIC* DAY-IN-MY-LIFE
body: i worked 3 jobs my first two years of a biomedical engineering undergrad then decided to pick up biochem during my second year even tho i had never done chem before. i was so tired i was seeing spots, couldn’t concentrate and once passed out on a train. best thing i did was cut back and focus on only 1-2 jobs. then take summer and winter subjects so i could perform well in uni without burning out. u don’t have to give everything up but SPACE IT OUT. because you can (and probs will) get physically sick and that WILL push you behind. the people i’ve met who perform the best are smart with their study. if you need to sleep then sleep, because when you’re that tired you won’t remember it anyway and it’s useless work. u might be fine now… but last year undergrad is when that burnout will start to hit HARD.
author: @Zoe-no1sg

title: HARVARD PRE-MED *REALISTIC* DAY-IN-MY-LIFE
body: Pre Med is BRUTAL. nobody talks about the stress this causes. constantly trying to keep a 3.7+ score a 515+ on the mcat, hundreds of hours of volunteering, research, and keeping friends.

title: HARVARD PRE-MED *REALISTIC* DAY-IN-MY-LIFE
body: And that`s how I got depression and anxiety. I study Applied Math and Comuter Science at MIPT but my stupid struggle hustle made me hate it. I am slowly regaining enjoyment from studying but it makes me sad because it feels like I used to know how to work hard play hard at school but fell for this hustle be productive mentality in uni and it was a huge mistake.
author: @MaySpencer

title: HARVARD PRE-MED *REALISTIC* DAY-IN-MY-LIFE
body: I wanted to say thank you for what you do. I’ve been hospitalized 7 times since I was 25. And I only met one nurse who was rude and had an attitude about everything and she was like this with everyone. But hospitalized 7 times and some of those times for over a week, that’s a lot of nurses, and you guys all remain so calm, and are the sweetest people on earth, idk how you guys do it, even with patients giving you a bad time. I’ve never said the words thank you so much in my life, but in the hospital. I love you all and thank you guys for taking care of us sick people, who need you there for us this goes to the doctors also.

title: Boston University Campus Tour 2020
body:
BU Virtual Tour, East Campus -> West Campus
1. Best dining hall - Bay State
2. Right across from MIT and Harvard
3. Commonwealth Ave - Train there and nice central campus
4. Radio Station
5. Mars Chapel - official central campus

6. BU Beach - Central Camp
7. George Sherman Union - Central Camp, where big events are hosted at
- dining point, library
8. A lot of stuff are integrated within campus (west campus side, but true for parts around campus)
- ex. Target, Chipotle
9. BU bus - free modes of transportation
10. BU gym - nice and big
- 7 basketball court and olympic sized swimming pool
11. BU hockey arena ( very good team)
12. Nickerson Field - a nice filed to chill and workout in

Thanks for the info Joshua!

title: Boston University Campus Tour 2020
body: Greetings from a BU alumnus (1987), in Tokyo. Very well done! Brought back a lot of memories to me. The myth of not stepping on the BU seal in front of Marsh Chapel?
